root_text.sn48.36:2.2: Yaṁ kho, bhikkhave, kāyikaṁ sukhaṁ, kāyikaṁ sātaṁ, kāyasamphassajaṁ sukhaṁ sātaṁ vedayitaṁ—
root_text.sn48.36:3.2: Yaṁ kho, bhikkhave, kāyikaṁ dukkhaṁ, kāyikaṁ asātaṁ, kāyasamphassajaṁ dukkhaṁ asātaṁ vedayitaṁ—
root_text.sn48.36:4.2: Yaṁ kho, bhikkhave, cetasikaṁ sukhaṁ, cetasikaṁ sātaṁ, manosamphassajaṁ sukhaṁ sātaṁ vedayitaṁ—
root_text.sn48.36:5.2: Yaṁ kho, bhikkhave, cetasikaṁ dukkhaṁ, cetasikaṁ asātaṁ, manosamphassajaṁ dukkhaṁ asātaṁ vedayitaṁ—
root_text.sn48.36:6.2: Yaṁ kho, bhikkhave, kāyikaṁ vā cetasikaṁ vā nevasātaṁ nāsātaṁ vedayitaṁ—

translation_text.sn48.36:1.3: The faculties of pleasure, pain, happiness, sadness, and equanimity.
translation_text.sn48.36:2.1: And what is the faculty of pleasure?
translation_text.sn48.36:2.2: Physical enjoyment, physical pleasure, the enjoyable, pleasant feeling that’s born from physical contact.
translation_text.sn48.36:2.3: This is called the faculty of pleasure.
translation_text.sn48.36:3.1: And what is the faculty of pain?
translation_text.sn48.36:3.2: Physical pain, physical unpleasantness, the painful, unpleasant feeling that’s born from physical contact.
translation_text.sn48.36:3.3: This is called the faculty of pain.
translation_text.sn48.36:4.1: And what is the faculty of happiness?
translation_text.sn48.36:4.2: Mental enjoyment, mental pleasure, the enjoyable, pleasant feeling that’s born from mind contact.
translation_text.sn48.36:4.3: This is called the faculty of happiness.
translation_text.sn48.36:5.1: And what is the faculty of sadness?
translation_text.sn48.36:5.2: Mental pain, mental displeasure, the painful, unpleasant feeling that’s born from mind contact.
translation_text.sn48.36:5.3: This is called the faculty of sadness.
translation_text.sn48.36:6.1: And what is the faculty of equanimity?
translation_text.sn48.36:6.2: Neither pleasant nor unpleasant feeling, whether physical or mental.
translation_text.sn48.36:6.3: This is the faculty of equanimity.
